message: So my partner and I had *that* conversation last week—the one where I'd mentioned keeping documents in a waterproof folder, and they'd kind of nodded and moved on. Then a pipe burst in the basement (minor, caught quickly, but still), and suddenly they were asking where we kept the insurance papers. I got to have the most satisfying, gentle 'I told you so' moment of my life. No gloating—just relief that we actually *knew* where things were. We spent an hour that evening making a simple list of what matters most and where it lives. Nothing fancy. Turns out there's something oddly comforting about knowing you've got your own back, even in small ways. Have you and your household ever had that click-moment where something finally felt worth doing?
